FR-001.title: Title the living-specs view Living Specs and the settings view Settings & Feedback, without changing any view id

FR-002.title: Render individual spec rows collapsed by default; Active group expanded, Completed and Archived collapsed

FR-003.title: Omit zero-item spec groups; keep group order Active, Completed, Archived with counts

FR-004.title: Preserve collapse-all / expand-all behavior for individual spec rows

FR-005.title: Use themed editor icons for functional and status concepts; reserve custom art for product identity and provider marks

FR-006.title: Render no emoji characters in any tree label, description, or tooltip

FR-007.title: Communicate every status by at least one non-color signal

FR-008.title: Render coherent per-document status icons regardless of parent spec lifecycle

FR-009.title: Resolve the provider icon through a single testable resolver that agrees with the provider display name

FR-010.title: Fall back to a neutral chat icon for an unrecognized host editor or unknown provider

FR-011.title: Show at most four actions in the Specs title bar: Filter, Sort, More Actions, New Spec

FR-012.title: Prefill the filter input with the active query; treat an empty submission as clearing the filter

FR-013.title: Expose collapse/expand, install, and upgrade through More Actions and keep them in the Command Palette

FR-014.title: Present the same actions in the same order in the hover More Actions submenu and the right-click menu

FR-015.title: Isolate delete in a final, separate group in both spec-row menus

FR-016.title: Show at most two inline actions on a spec row: Resume when eligible, and More Actions

FR-017.title: Offer both reveal actions on every file-backed Living Specs and Steering row, resolving exact paths

FR-018.title: Offer no reveal action on a row whose file does not exist

FR-019.title: Restrict destructive steering actions to generated steering documents

FR-020.title: Nest each missing-rule create action under its provider Project or User group and name the real filename

FR-021.title: Build the Steering root in one explicit order and use User consistently

FR-022.title: Open the configuration file when the Configuration row is clicked, while still listing setting groups

FR-023.title: Render an informative row for Living Specs disabled, empty, or error states

FR-024.title: Present friendly title-cased status text in tooltips; expose no raw lifecycle keys

FR-025.title: Derive document tooltip paths from the stored path, not the display label

FR-026.title: Preserve every command id, view id, stored setting, lifecycle rule, multi-select, filter, sort, and Resume gate

FR-027.title: Keep sidebar rendering native: no webview, no custom styling, no hand-rolled hover/focus/selection/menus

FR-028.title: Cover the contribution manifest and tree presentation logic with automated regression tests

intent: Make the SpecKit sidebar read as one coherent, quiet, VS Code-native product without changing any behavior
expectations: No webview, custom CSS, or hand-rolled hover/focus/selection/menu rendering
No breaking changes to command ids, view ids, stored settings, lifecycle rules, multi-select, filter/sort, or Resume gates
No redesign of the spec viewer or editor webviews
No new icon package; no renaming files or directories on disk
Screenshots are a manual surface — not captured by this change
context: area: src/features/specs (spec + living-specs tree providers, spec commands)
area: src/features/steering (steering tree provider, provider icons)
constraint: stay native to VS Code TreeView semantics
approach: Presentation-only pass across package.json contributions and the four tree providers: retitle views, collapse spec rows by default, swap decorative SVGs for themed Codicons, extract a pure provider-icon resolver and a friendly-status formatter, consolidate the Specs toolbar behind a More Actions QuickPick, align hover and right-click menus with Delete isolated, and rebuild the Steering root in explicit order. A manifest-parsing test plus tree-presentation tests land first as the safety net.

decisions.decision: Lock the contributed manifest with a package.json-parsing test before changing anything
decisions.why: Every acceptance criterion is a statement about the manifest or a tree item field, and none is covered today
decisions.rejected: Integration tests against a real VS Code host — slow and cannot assert a ceiling on title actions

decisions.decision: Extract provider-icon selection into a pure resolver returning a discriminated icon key
decisions.why: The icon path disagrees with the label path today — an unknown IDE Chat host renders Copilot branding
decisions.rejected: Fixing the ternary in place inside the TreeItem constructor — untestable and drifts again

decisions.decision: Wibey resolves to an explicit, documented neutral chat Codicon
decisions.why: No official Wibey mark ships and inventing one is worse than an honest neutral glyph; an explicit table row turns an accidental fallback into a documented one
decisions.rejected: Reusing the generic robot glyph — indistinguishable from an unknown provider

decisions.decision: Consolidate the Specs toolbar behind a More Actions QuickPick built by a pure composition function
decisions.why: Makes the picker composition unit-testable against every context combination, which a declarative submenu cannot be

decisions.decision: Filter clears on an empty submission; the clear-filter icon leaves the title bar
decisions.why: Buys back a title slot without losing a capability; the command stays registered for the palette and the no-match welcome view
decisions.rejected: Dropping Sort to More Actions instead — the plan requires Sort to stay a direct title action

decisions.decision: Document status icons stop depending on the parent spec lifecycle
decisions.why: Expanding a completed spec currently shows iconless rows that read as nothing happened; the parent beaker already carries the parent state
decisions.rejected: Muting child icons under an archived parent

decisions.decision: A missing spec document gets its own context value rather than reusing the per-step one
decisions.why: Menus gate on the context value, so a distinct value is the only way to stop offering open and reveal actions against a file that is not there
decisions.rejected: Leaving the per-step context value and letting the reveal handler error at click time

decisions.decision: The Specs More Actions picker is a QuickPick composed by a pure function, not a contributed submenu
decisions.why: The composition is then unit-testable against every context combination, and the collapse entry can reflect the current tree state as one line

plan.summary: Presentation-only redesign across the manifest and four tree providers, with two pure modules extracted for the provider-icon and friendly-status correctness bugs
plan.key_finding: The provider icon and the provider display name are computed by two separate code paths that disagree for an unknown IDE Chat host — the icon falls through to Copilot while the label says IDE Chat

tasks.summary: 38 tasks across 10 phases — foundational safety net (manifest lock + two pure modules), then one phase per user story in priority order, ending in docs and a full verification gate

implement.summary: Presentation-only sidebar redesign: renamed views, collapsed spec rows, Codicon icon system, tested provider-icon resolver, four-action toolbar behind a More Actions picker, aligned hover/right-click menus with Delete isolated, rebuilt Steering root, full reveal coverage, and refreshed docs

T001.did: Added a package.json-parsing manifest lock test covering view titles, the four-action Specs toolbar, spec-row menu grouping with Delete isolated, lifecycle when-clauses, and reveal eligibility

T002.did: Created the pure provider-icon resolver with host-IDE detection and a documented neutral fallback

T003.did: Created the pure friendly-status formatter for spec and document statuses

T004.did: Tested the provider-icon resolver across every provider and host, asserting label/icon agreement and no Copilot fallback in an unknown host

T005.did: Tested the friendly-status formatter for every canonical status and an unknown value

T006.did: Renamed the contributed view titles to Living Specs and Settings & Feedback, leaving ids, when-clauses, and visibility untouched

T007.did: Manifest test asserts the new view titles

T010.did: Applied the command-title copy table across 25 commands without changing an id, and added the More Actions command

T013.did: Manifest test asserts every retitled command and that no title carries an emoji

T020.did: Reduced the Specs title bar to Filter, Sort, More Actions, and New Spec; moved clear-filter, collapse/expand, install, and upgrade out while keeping them contributed

T023.did: Manifest test asserts the four-action ceiling and that every removed title action is still a contributed, palette-reachable command

T024.did: Rebuilt the spec-row context menu on the same five groups as the hover submenu with Delete isolated in 5_danger, and extended reveal eligibility to orphan living specs and every file-backed Steering row

T027.did: Manifest test asserts hover/right-click parity, Delete isolation, the full reveal matrix, and that missing rows expose no reveal

T008.did: Replaced the Specs group SVGs with pulse / passed-tinted check / archive Codicons and rewrote the group tooltips

T009.did: Replaced every generic Steering category SVG with a themed Codicon, keeping the moss product mark and the official provider marks

T011.did: Rewrote spec-row tooltips as multi-line friendly status plus last activity, dropped the leading bullet from the description, and derived document tooltips from the stored path

T012.did: Standardized the agent tools count and made the missing-rule tooltips name the configured provider's steering filename

T014.did: Rewrote the provider icon as a thin adapter over the pure resolver

T015.did: Defaulted individual spec rows to collapsed

T016.did: Seeded the all-collapsed context key at registration and in the activation reset so the affordance reads correctly on a fresh window

T017.did: Document status icons now derive from the document's own state regardless of parent lifecycle, with a distinct context value for a missing document

T018.did: Extended the spec-explorer tests for collapsed defaults, Codicon groups, friendly tooltips, and document icons under every parent lifecycle

T019.did: Added the pure More Actions composition function

T021.did: Registered the More Actions picker, made an empty filter submission clear the filter, and compacted the sort picker copy

T022.did: Tested the More Actions composition across every context combination plus the sort picker labels and the filter clear-on-empty path

T025.did: Related-document rows now carry a file URI so the open and reveal actions resolve, and missing documents expose neither

T026.did: Added Living Specs provider tests for the disabled, empty, orphan, and missing-capability states, and corrected the empty-state copy

T028.did: Built the Steering root in one explicit order and renamed the spec-kit section to SpecKit Project Files

T029.did: Moved the missing rule create actions into the provider's Project and User groups

T030.did: The Companion Configuration row now opens the config file on click while still listing its setting groups

T031.did: Removed the artificial loading flicker from the Steering refresh

T032.did: Added Steering provider tests for root order, nested create actions with real provider filenames, the Companion node, and the flicker-free refresh

T033.did: Deleted the unreferenced decorative icon assets and updated NOTICE to stop attributing artwork the extension no longer ships

T034.did: Rewrote the sidebar reference for the new view names, four-action toolbar, More Actions picker, Codicon icon system, provider-icon table with Wibey's documented fallback, aligned menus, and the rebuilt Steering hierarchy

T035.did: Rewrote the README's Sidebar at a Glance for the new names, collapsed-by-default rows, four-action toolbar, and aligned menus

T036.did: Added the user-facing changelog entry under Unreleased

T037.did: Committed the design plan this feature implements

T038.did: Ran compile, the full Jest suite (1456 tests, 107 suites), and the packaging build — all green

concerns.note: Screenshots could not be re-rendered — capturing the sidebar needs a human running VS Code, so docs/screenshots/ was deliberately left untouched rather than shipping a stale or faked image.

last_action: all 38 tasks done — compile, 1456 tests, and the packaging build green
